Investigation complete and within budget — five Council-verified findings (001–004 HIGH, 006 MEDIUM), with the one bounce (005) self-corrected into 006.

## Investigative narrative — Case VANKO-AUTO (Standard Forensic Case 2)

The evidence set is a forensically sound, **MD5-verified physical image of a Microsoft Surface 3** ("Live Physical" acquisition by examiner Ovie Carroll under case 20161104, AccessData FTK Imager — **F-001**), accompanied by a **CYLR live-response triage archive** of the same host's volume. `mmls` confirms a **UEFI/GPT disk layout** (EFI system partition, Microsoft reserved partition, recovery, and a large ~110 GiB basic-data partition at sector offset 1411072 — **F-002**), and `fls` confirms that large partition is an **NTFS volume hosting a Windows operating system** (`$MFT`/`$Boot`/`$LogFile`, plus `Windows`, `ProgramData`, `Program Files`, `Users`); the presence of `Windows.old` and `$Windows.~GWX`/`$Windows.~WS` indicates an in-place Windows upgrade/reinstall occurred on this volume (**F-003**). The companion archive `vanko-c-drive.CYLR.7z` is a CYLR-convention triage collection of a Windows volume referenced as drive **G**, preserving forensic directory trees across **6,576 files / 1,750 folders** (**F-004**), and the on-disk `Users` directory enumerates standard template profiles plus two named profiles — **`PC User`** and **`defaultprinter`** — and a `Default.migrated` entry consistent with the upgrade evidence (**F-006**).

**Scope discipline:** every verified finding is provenance/structure-level — image integrity, partition/filesystem mapping, triage-archive scope, and profile-directory existence. None asserts human attribution, account ownership, login activity, program execution, data transfer, malware, or intent; those would require artifact-level analysis (registry hives, event logs, $MFT timelines, prefetch/amcache) not yet performed. Notably present but **not yet investigated** leads for a follow-up pass: a deleted `runme.cmd`/`runme.cmd.digest` and `version.txt` at the NTFS root, and a `uTorrent.exe` under the `PC User` profile in the CYLR set — all observed only as filenames, claimed as nothing more.

Receipts chain GENESIS → 001 → 002 → 003 → 004 → 006 under `council/receipts/`.
